title: humboltia barberiana
intro: Humboltia barberiana, also known as the "Puebla Marigold" or "Mexican Marigold," is a perennial flowering plant native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in treating various health issues. The plant's extracts have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing antioxidant properties that protect against cell damage. Humboltia barberiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
